description:
In CleanupStore.cleanup_old_articles(), the DELETE query has a faulty condition:

Usage: crystal [command] [switches] [program file] [--] [arguments]

Command:
    init                     generate a new project
    build                    build an executable
    clear_cache              clear the compiler cache
    docs                     generate documentation
    env                      print Crystal environment information
    eval                     eval code from args or standard input
    i/interactive            starts interactive Crystal
    play                     starts Crystal playground server
    run (default)            build and run program
    spec                     build and run specs (in spec directory)
    tool                     run a tool
    help, --help, -h         show this help
    version, --version, -v   show version

Run a command followed by --help to see command specific information, ex:
    crystal <command> --help

The condition  is problematic:
1.  - correctly targets unclustered items
2.  - incorrectly targets ALL cluster representative items (where cluster_id equals its own id)

This means ALL cluster representatives older than cutoff are deleted, regardless of cluster membership. The intended behavior should be to preserve representatives of multi-item clusters.

A representative item with cluster_id = id means:
- It's the anchor item of its cluster (not a member)
- It typically has the latest/most relevant content in the cluster
- Deleting it breaks clustering display

The fix should distinguish between:
- Items with cluster_id = id (singletons/representatives) - should preserve if cluster has other members
- Items with cluster_id IS NULL - should delete based on age

Alternatively, the condition should be  only, or the query should verify cluster membership before deletion.

Code location: src/storage/cleanup_store.cr - cleanup_old_articles method
title: Bug: cleanup_old_articles cluster_id condition logic error
